problem_addressed: Semi-supervised key information extraction faces underestimation of tail class confidence in long-tailed distributions and difficulty achieving intra-class compactness and inter-class separability of tail features.
core_claim: CRMSP achieves state-of-the-art performance in semi-supervised KIE by combining Class-Rebalancing Pseudo-Labeling and Merged Semantic Pseudo-Labeling modules to address long-tailed distribution challenges.
key_contributions: Class-Rebalancing Pseudo-Labeling (CRP) module introduces reweighting factor to rebalance pseudo-labels, increasing attention to tail classes
Merged Semantic Pseudo-Labeling (MSP) module clusters tail features using Merged Prototypes to improve semantic pseudo-labeling
Overall framework achieves 3.24% F1-score improvement over state-of-the-art on CORD benchmark
novelty: Direct rebalancing of pseudo-labels based on class distributions (vs. complex reweighting losses in prior work) and Merged Prototypes approach for tail feature clustering (vs. unrepresentative prototypes in DASO)
method_summary: CRMSP utilizes a base model with labeled/unlabeled data processing, where CRP reweights pseudo-labels using EMA-based class distribution estimates and β factor, while MSP generates merged semantic pseudo-labels via Merged Prototypes and contrastive loss.
key_innovation: Reweighting factor β that reverses class distribution predictions (β = Normalize(M(˜p))) to boost tail class confidence during pseudo-label filtering
assumptions: Smoothness assumption: close data points in high-density regions have similar outputs
Unlabeled data distribution correlates with labeled data distribution
hyperparameters: Batch size=4, µ=1.0, Learning rate=1e-5, Tproto=1.0, EMA momentum=0.999, λun=0.1, λctr=0.1, τ=0.95, K=5, Training epoch=256, Batch size=64, Learning rate=0.03, Weight decay=5e-4
headline_result: CRMSP achieved 91.30% F1-score on CORD with 10% labeled data using LayoutLMv3, close to fully-supervised result of 93.30%
ablations: RP (Reweighting Pseudo-Labels) improved F1-score from 66.56% to 67.21% on FUNSD and 84.05% to 84.76% on CORD
Lctr (Contrastive Loss) further improved F1-score to 67.75% on FUNSD and 85.36% on CORD
MP (Merged Prototypes) achieved final improvement to 71.12% on FUNSD and 89.59% on CORD
RP significantly improved F1-score for tail classes on CORD, especially for classes with smaller sample sizes
improvement_over_baseline: CRMSP improved F1-score by 5.81% compared to FixMatch on FUNSD with 5% labeled data using LayoutLMv2, improved F1-score by 4.32% compared to FixMatch on CORD with 10% labeled data using LayoutLMv3, and improved F1-score by 1.20% compared to SimMatch on FUNSD with 5% labeled data using LayoutLMv2
stated_limitations: The paper does not explicitly state limitations in the provided abstract and highlights.
unstated_weaknesses: No explicit discussion of uncertainty quantification in the pseudo-labeling process
Limited information on handling varying degrees of label noise in unlabeled data
No details on how the approach performs with high uncertainty scenarios
Unclear how the method handles uncertainty in feature space for tail classes
threats_to_validity: Potential sensitivity to hyperparameters related to pseudo-labeling thresholds
Limited evaluation on datasets with different uncertainty characteristics
Unclear generalizability to domains with different uncertainty patterns
reproducibility: medium (paper appears to be recent with arXiv reference, but no information on code/data availability)
open_questions: How does the uncertainty in pseudo-labels affect the model's performance?
What is the optimal way to quantify and utilize uncertainty in the pseudo-labeling process?
How does the approach perform when the uncertainty in unlabeled data is high?
Can the method be extended to explicitly model uncertainty in the pseudo-label selection?
future_work: The paper does not explicitly mention future work in the provided abstract and highlights.
gaps_this_enables: Integration of uncertainty-aware techniques into the pseudo-labeling process
Development of methods to explicitly model uncertainty in semi-supervised learning for long-tailed distributions
Research on uncertainty quantification in merged semantic pseudo-labeling
relevance_to_topic: While CRMSP addresses pseudo-labeling challenges in semi-supervised learning, it lacks explicit uncertainty-aware mechanisms that are central to the project topic.
best_result: CRMSP achieved 91.30% F1-score on CORD with 10% labeled data using LayoutLMv3, close to fully-supervised result of 93.30%
limitations_stated: The paper does not explicitly state limitations in the provided abstract and highlights.
title: CRMSP: A Semi-supervised Approach for Key Information Extraction with Class-Rebalancing and Merged Semantic Pseudo-Labeling
